message:
feat(hello): full Redis 7.2-compatible HELLO response with client identity fields

Overhaul the HELLO command response to match real Redis 7.2 server behavior,
improving compatibility with clients that parse server info fields beyond the
basic server/version/proto triple.

Runtime changes (fr-runtime):
- build_hello_response() now accepts client_id and emits all 7 standard fields:
  server="redis", version="7.2.0", proto=N, id=<client_id>, mode="standalone",
  role="master", modules=[] — matching the wire format real Redis returns
- Replaced "frankenredis"/"0.1.0" identifiers with "redis"/"7.2.0" so that
  client libraries (redis-py, ioredis, jedis) that sniff server identity
  during HELLO negotiation treat us as a standard Redis instance
- WrongArity error messages now use lowercase command names ("get" not "GET")
  for parity with Redis 7.x behavior

Conformance changes (fr-conformance):
- Added AnyInteger variant to ExpectedFrame enum to support wildcard matching
  on non-deterministic fields like client id
- Introduced frame_matches_expected() recursive matcher that handles AnyInteger
  wildcards inside nested arrays, replacing the previous strict equality check
- Updated all HELLO-related test fixtures (core_client, core_connection,
  fr_p2c_004_acl_journey) to expect the expanded 14-element response with
  id/mode/role/modules fields
- Updated WrongArity and CLUSTER test expectations to use lowercase command names

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
